title: BLE transport: data race on nus_tx_notify_enabled global
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/ble_ipsp_transport.c
Lines: 309, 314, 460

The global variable nus_tx_notify_enabled is:
- Written in nus_ccc_cfg_changed() callback (line 314) without any synchronization
- Read in lichen_ble_slip_send() at line 460 without synchronization

This is a data race. While the variable is a bool and writes may be atomic on many platforms, this is undefined behavior per C standard.

Fix: Either protect with transport_state.lock mutex, use an atomic type, or document platform assumptions.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced bool nus_tx_notify_enabled with atomic_t, using atomic_set() for writes and atomic_get() for reads to eliminate data race
